title: Mayor issues 45 vetoes to Common Council's amendments on Syracuse city budget
summary: Syracuse Mayor Ben Walsh issued 45 vetoes on May 19, 2025 against Common Council amendments to the city budget, rejecting cuts to police, fire, code enforcement, and Parks departments totaling $16M. The Council had commissioned external analysis recommending $26M in cost reductions across the $348M budget, but Walsh argued the cuts harm public safety and city services; 37 amendments worth $3.4M were accepted.
department_refs: Department of Parks, Recreation and Youth Programs

political_signal.description: Mayor exercising veto authority against Common Council budget amendments; Council signaling fiscal discipline and cost containment scrutiny of executive budget proposal; potential for veto override votes requiring 6+ council votes.
